key_insight: Phonon Hall viscosity at zero magnetic field provides a symmetry-sensitive fingerprint that distinguishes altermagnets from ferromagnets and conventional antiferromagnets.
engineering_relevance: The result suggests a magneto-acoustic route to detect broken symmetries and topological features in insulating altermagnets through strain–phonon response measurements.
method: Derive the Hall viscosity tensor response for altermagnetic systems at zero magnetic field.
Compute viscosity microscopically for d-wave and g-wave altermagnet models.
Link strong viscosity features to gapped Dirac points, Lifshitz transitions, and strain-space Berry curvature monopoles.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports analytical and microscopic model calculations showing that specific Hall-viscosity tensor components uniquely identify altermagnets and are highly sensitive to electronic structure changes.
watch_item: Experimental extraction may be challenging because the signal depends on subtle tensor components and may require clean insulating samples with well-resolved magneto-acoustic measurements.
